text: With the influx of globalisation and digitalisation the Christian church is flooded with vast amounts of information and religious literature which challenge orthodox doctrine. New age teachings which distort biblical truths have become increasingly complex and therefore pose a great challenge to societal stability, the unity of the Body of Christ and to the spiritual foundation of individual Christians. This study deals with the problems faced by the church in relation to the identification and combating of heresies in the modern age in relation to the preservation of the faith. The descriptive qualitative method with literature study was used in the research in order to study the contemporary heresies from the perspective of spiritual reality. The results of the study show that the church as the main preserver of faith should take action in order to guarantee an in-depth theological training for the Christians, and that the church must make real the defensive role of preserving the faith and protecting the believers from being misled. The study also underlines the importance of the church's role in providing the Christians with enough discernment to cope with the spiritual challenges of the digitalisation in order to maintain the unity and purity of the Christian faith.
explanation: The piece is a polished, generic academic abstract with no distinctive personal voice or concrete data, a pattern commonly produced by AI. This makes AI authorship plausible but not certain.
